mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.11% is near the zero lower bound, constraining further monetary easing. As inflation (CPIAUCSL at 231.485) remains elevated relative to the Fed's 2% target, market expectations shift toward eventual rate normalization. This forward-looking tightening bias pushes 10-year Treasury yields upward over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.11% near ZLB as of May 2013
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with persistent inflation above target
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over the 20-day window despite FEDFUNDS remaining near ZLB and CPIAUCSL above 2.5% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS at 135,637k) is growing steadily while unemployment (UNRATE at 7.6%) remains elevated. This labor market slack signals the Fed will maintain accommodative policy longer than markets initially priced, depressing long-term yields. A decline in UNRATE below 7.4% over the next 60 trading days would trigger expectations of imminent tightening, reversing this effect.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 7.4% within 60 trading days
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.6% as of May 2013 with ongoing payroll gains
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market slack
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 25 basis points over the 60-day window despite UNRATE remaining above 7.4% and PAYEMS growth continuing, or the interaction effect (joint UNRATE-PAYEMS signal) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS at 421,147m) and housing starts (HOUST at 853k) both show robust demand momentum. This broad-based demand strength signals accelerating economic growth, which raises inflation expectations and forces the Fed to normalize rates sooner. 10-year Treasury yields rise as markets price in faster rate liftoff.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 853k annualized starts, RSAFS at 421.1B in April 2013
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines despite both RSAFS and HOUST remaining above their 3-month moving averages, or the joint interaction signal produces negative Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO at 98.67) is below its 2017 baseline, indicating slack in productive capacity. This supply-side weakness limits inflation pressures and allows the Fed to maintain low rates. 10-year Treasury yields decline as markets price in extended monetary accommodation.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO remains below 100 for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.67 as of May 2013, below 2017=100 baseline
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with persistent supply slack
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 20 basis points over the 20-day window despite INDPRO remaining below 100, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1 at 13,746.2B) is accelerating from post-crisis lows. When quarterly GDP growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, markets reprrice the terminal Fed funds rate upward, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher. This mechanism activates only when growth momentum crosses the 2.5% threshold.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 2.5% annualized
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over the 60-day window in quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 20-quarter rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising while the federal funds rate remains at 0.11%, creating deeply negative real rates. This regime of financial repression is unsustainable and signals imminent Fed tightening. Markets front-run this normalization by pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward. The mechanism strengthens when CPI growth accelerates above 2.0% year-over-year.
evidence_refs: fact:Real rates deeply negative with FEDFUNDS at 0.11% and inflation elevated
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over the 5-day window despite CPI YoY growth exceeding 2.0% and FEDFUNDS at ZLB, or the interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 6-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment at 7.6% is elevated relative to the Fed's estimated natural rate of ~5.5%. This slack allows the Fed to maintain accommodative policy without inflation risk. As long as UNRATE remains above 7.0%, the Fed signals commitment to low rates, suppressing 10-year Treasury yields. The mechanism reverses when UNRATE falls below 7.0%, signaling tightening ahead.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 30 basis points over the 20-day window despite UNRATE remaining above 7.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST at 853k) are accelerating from post-crisis lows toward normalized levels (~1.2M). This housing recovery signals strong demand and wealth effects that boost consumption and inflation expectations. Markets price in faster Fed normalization, pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward. The mechanism activates when HOUST exceeds 800k annualized.
candidate_trigger: HOUST exceeds 800k annualized for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 853k in April 2013, above 800k threshold
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over the 60-day window despite HOUST remaining above 800k and RSAFS growth continuing, or the joint housing-sales interaction produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.
